name: Burkina Faso
experiences: Grand Mosque and old quarter walks in Bobo-Dioulasso
Karfiguéla Falls and Sindou Peaks near Banfora
Ruins of Loropéni UNESCO day trip
avoid: June to September is the wettest stretch, so laterite roads around Tiébélé, Sindou, and Loropéni become slower and muddier.

Safety advisory: independent trips are usually limited to the Ouagadougou-Bobo-Dioulasso-Banfora-Tiébélé corridor in daylight; avoid the far north, east, and border zones unless you have current local guidance and have rechecked travel advisories.
Cash is still essential for taxis, community guides, and attraction fees; card acceptance is mostly limited to larger Ouagadougou hotels.
stopoverNote: Ouagadougou (OUA) is the clear gateway. The cleanest first-time loop is Ouagadougou → Tiébélé → Bobo-Dioulasso → Banfora, using taxis for the southbound Tiébélé leg and Rakieta or TCV coaches for the western intercity stretches.

cities.name: Ouagadougou
cities.notes: Capital base for the National Museum, Moro-Naba court ceremony, craft shopping, major hotels, and late-February FESPACO screenings in odd-numbered years.

cities.name: Tiébélé
cities.notes: Kassena country stop for the painted royal court, rural compounds, and a slower overnight close to the Ghana border.

cities.name: Bobo-Dioulasso
cities.notes: Burkina Faso's most atmospheric city for the clay Grand Mosque, old Kibidwé quarter, music history, and excellent market food.

cities.name: Banfora
cities.notes: Southwest base for Cascades de Karfiguéla, Domes de Fabedougou, the Sindou Peaks, and the longer UNESCO run toward Loropéni.

itinerary.sim: Orange Burkina Faso is the easiest all-route pick for Ouaga, Bobo, and Banfora, with patchier but usable coverage toward Tiébélé; expect roughly ₹420–₹1,120 for a prepaid SIM or eSIM bundle with enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and bookings.

days.theme: Settle into Burkina Faso with museum context, central boulevards, and an easy first-night food circuit.

activities.name: Airport taxi from Ouagadougou Airport into the city center or Ouaga 2000
Musée National du Burkina Faso entry
Cathédrale de l'Immaculée-Conception and central boulevard walk
Village Artisanal de Ouagadougou craft-market browse
Taxi hops between the museum, craft village, and dinner

days.theme: Use a second capital day for living traditions, green space, and a festival screening if your dates match FESPACO.

activities.name: Moro-Naba ceremony visit in Gounghin (Friday morning viewing)
Parc Urbain Bangr-Weoogo entry
Musée de la Musique entry
FESPACO public film-screening ticket when the festival is on
City taxi hops between Gounghin, Bangr-Weoogo, and the cinema halls

Tiébélé.name: Tiébélé
Tiébélé.note: Road conditions vary a lot after Po, so start early and avoid trying to do the round-trip from Ouaga in a rush during the rainy season.

days.theme: Travel south into Kassena country for one of West Africa's most distinctive living architectural sites.

activities.name: Shared taxi or hired car from Ouagadougou to Tiébélé via Po
Royal Court of Tiébélé community entry with mandatory local guide
Short local taxi or moto ride between the market area and the royal court
Evening village walk with a local host

days.theme: Cross west by coach, then spend the afternoon on Bobo's old mud-brick core and market streets.

activities.name: Rakieta or TCV coach from Ouagadougou to Bobo-Dioulasso
Guided visit to the Grand Mosque of Bobo-Dioulasso
Bobo old quarter and Kibidwé artisans area walk
Taxi hops between the station, mosque quarter, and your hotel

days.theme: Lean into Bobo's music-and-history side with museum time and a relaxed second city day.

activities.name: Musée Sogossira Sanon entry
Old railway station and Avenue de la Nation walk
Local guide or taxi run to the sacred fish quarter of Dafra
Evening live-music cover at a central maquis or hotel venue

Banfora.name: Banfora
Banfora.note: Banfora works best with your own taxi for waterfalls, domes, Sindou, or the long Loropéni run; public transport exists but makes the day much slower.

days.theme: Move south from Bobo for the classic Banfora nature loop of falls, domes, and sugar-cane country.

activities.name: Rakieta, TCV, or shared taxi from Bobo-Dioulasso to Banfora
Cascades de Karfiguéla entry
Domes de Fabedougou local guide fee
Taxi loop for Banfora, Karfiguéla, and Fabedougou

meals: Restaurant de l'Hôtel de la Comoé

days.theme: Use Banfora for the sandstone drama of Sindou and an unhurried southwest road day.

activities.name: Pics de Sindou entry
Mandatory local guide for the Sindou Peaks circuit
Taxi or shared-car run from Banfora to Sindou and back
Banfora market fruit and cane-juice stop

meals: Restaurant de l'Hôtel de la Comoé

days.theme: If you have a final extra day, stretch southwest for Burkina Faso's signature UNESCO fortification site.

activities.name: Private or shared vehicle from Banfora toward Gaoua and Loropéni
Ruins of Loropéni UNESCO site entry
Local guide at the Ruins of Loropéni

connections.method: Shared taxi via Po is the usual budget option, while a hired taxi or driver is far smoother if you want to keep the same day flexible
Most travelers return north toward Ouagadougou or Po first, then continue west by Rakieta or TCV coach; a direct private transfer is faster but expensive
Rakieta, TCV, and shared taxis all run this easy southwest corridor, with taxis saving the most time for side trips
Overnight or daytime coach with Rakieta/TCV is the simplest reset, while shared taxis are faster but less comfortable

extras: November to February is the sweet spot: the air is drier and cooler, waterfall and sandstone sites are easier to manage, and long road days are much less punishing than in the rains.
Security conditions in Burkina Faso can change quickly, so confirm the latest advice with your embassy and hotel before taking any road beyond the Ouaga-Bobo-Banfora-Tiébélé corridor.

FESPACO is usually held in late February of odd-numbered years; if your dates miss it, keep the Ouagadougou cinema line as optional and spend the extra time at Bangr-Weoogo or the craft village instead.
Banfora rewards an early start: Karfiguéla, Fabedougou, Sindou, and Loropéni all involve heat and long drives, and local taxis are easiest to negotiate the evening before.
Outside larger hotels, expect cash-only payments for bus tickets, guides, and attraction entries, and keep small notes ready for station porters and taxi changes.
